---
title: "ipython notebook tips and tricks for science research"
date: 2013-10-08
author: Jonathan Whitmore
draft: true
description: "Resources and notebooks from a seminar on using Python and the IPython Notebook for scientific research, covering clean code practices and the future of reproducible science."
categories: [python, science, tools, education]
---

David Lagattuta and I gave a seminar at CAS about using [python](http://www.python.org/), [ipython](http://ipython.org/) and the [ipython notebook](http://ipython.org/notebook.html). At the end of it we made 3 of our ipython notebooks available to all.

These notebooks have embedded in them examples of images, text, LaTeX, and even embedded YouTube screencasts that explain aspects of the notebook within the notebook itself.

	
- **ipython notebook: Future of Science**: [https://nbviewer.jupyter.org/5742826](https://nbviewer.jupyter.org/5742826)
	
- **Clean Code; Clear Code**: [https://nbviewer.jupyter.org/5742829](https://nbviewer.jupyter.org/5742829)
	
- **The Future of Science -- EXTRAS**: [https://nbviewer.jupyter.org/5742830](https://nbviewer.jupyter.org/5742830)


Here's a screencast that we embedded which shows some neat ipython notebook features:

{{< video //www.youtube.com/embed/1HSclOlW3nQ >}}

As always, I'm looking to improve my coding (and presentation) skills, so constructive criticism is requested.

Thanks!
